description_long: This bungalow features a single-story structure with a low-pitched, terracotta-tiled hip roof and stone-clad exterior walls. The front facade includes a small covered porch with a gabled entryway, supported by columns and flanked by two large windows. The stonework extends around the base and sides, giving the building a rustic yet sturdy appearance. The windows are symmetrically arranged on both the front and rear facades, with white frames and shutters. The surrounding area is paved with stone tiles, providing a neat and defined perimeter. The overall design is compact, emphasizing horizontal lines and a welcoming, residential character.
description: A single-story stone-clad bungalow with a terracotta hip roof, symmetrical windows, and a small covered front porch.
description_view_0: Front view showing the gabled porch entry, two large windows, and stone-paved perimeter.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the hip roof's extension and two side windows, with visible stone paving.
description_view_2: Rear view with a long facade, symmetrical windows, and a continuous stone base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirroring the other side, showing the hip roof and two windows.
